questions.question: Identify the correct architecture term-definition mapping.

Instruction Cycle -> unit performing arithmetic and logical operations in CPU
questions.explanation: Only 'ALU -> unit performing arithmetic and logical operations in CPU' is correctly mapped.

questions.question: A system design requirement states: instruction set philosophy with simple fixed-length instructions. Which concept should be selected?
questions.explanation: The requirement directly points to RISC.

questions.question: Which Computer Architecture concept best matches: instruction set philosophy with complex variable-length instructions?
questions.explanation: CISC is defined as: instruction set philosophy with complex variable-length instructions.

questions.question: What is the most accurate description of 'CISC'?
options: unit directing instruction execution and control signals
I/O method using separate address space for devices
instruction set philosophy with complex variable-length instructions
questions.explanation: CISC refers to: instruction set philosophy with complex variable-length instructions.

questions.question: 'CISC' belongs mainly to which architecture area?
questions.explanation: CISC is mainly part of Architecture Design.

questions.question: A system design requirement states: instruction set philosophy with complex variable-length instructions. Which concept should be selected?
questions.explanation: The requirement directly points to CISC.

questions.question: Which Computer Architecture concept best matches: separate memory paths for instructions and data?
questions.explanation: Harvard Architecture is defined as: separate memory paths for instructions and data.

questions.question: What is the most accurate description of 'Harvard Architecture'?
options: larger but slower cache than L1
condition causing pipeline stall or incorrect execution
separate memory paths for instructions and data
CPU executing instructions as operands become available
questions.explanation: Harvard Architecture refers to: separate memory paths for instructions and data.

questions.question: 'Harvard Architecture' belongs mainly to which architecture area?
questions.explanation: Harvard Architecture is mainly part of Architecture Design.

questions.question: A system design requirement states: separate memory paths for instructions and data. Which concept should be selected?
questions.explanation: The requirement directly points to Harvard Architecture.

questions.question: Which Computer Architecture concept best matches: single memory path shared by instructions and data?
questions.explanation: Von Neumann Architecture is defined as: single memory path shared by instructions and data.

questions.question: What is the most accurate description of 'Von Neumann Architecture'?
options: byte order used for storing multi-byte data
number of CPU cycles executed per second
I/O method using separate address space for devices
single memory path shared by instructions and data
questions.explanation: Von Neumann Architecture refers to: single memory path shared by instructions and data.

questions.question: 'Von Neumann Architecture' belongs mainly to which architecture area?
questions.explanation: Von Neumann Architecture is mainly part of Architecture Design.

questions.question: A system design requirement states: single memory path shared by instructions and data. Which concept should be selected?
questions.explanation: The requirement directly points to Von Neumann Architecture.

questions.question: Which Computer Architecture concept best matches: control unit design using microinstructions in control memory?
questions.explanation: Microprogramming is defined as: control unit design using microinstructions in control memory.

questions.question: What is the most accurate description of 'Microprogramming'?
options: storing most significant byte at lowest memory address
technique extending memory using disk space
control unit design using microinstructions in control memory
pipeline issue due to hardware resource conflict
questions.explanation: Microprogramming refers to: control unit design using microinstructions in control memory.

questions.question: 'Microprogramming' belongs mainly to which architecture area?
questions.explanation: Microprogramming is mainly part of Architecture Design.

questions.question: A system design requirement states: control unit design using microinstructions in control memory. Which concept should be selected?
questions.explanation: The requirement directly points to Microprogramming.

questions.question: Which Computer Architecture concept best matches: byte order used for storing multi-byte data?
questions.explanation: Endianness is defined as: byte order used for storing multi-byte data.

questions.question: What is the most accurate description of 'Endianness'?
options: instruction set philosophy with complex variable-length instructions
byte order used for storing multi-byte data
technique guessing branch outcome to improve pipeline flow
small high-speed memory between CPU and RAM
questions.explanation: Endianness refers to: byte order used for storing multi-byte data.

questions.question: 'Endianness' belongs mainly to which architecture area?
questions.explanation: Endianness is mainly part of Architecture Design.

questions.question: A system design requirement states: byte order used for storing multi-byte data. Which concept should be selected?
questions.explanation: The requirement directly points to Endianness.

questions.question: Which Computer Architecture concept best matches: storing most significant byte at lowest memory address?
questions.explanation: Big Endian is defined as: storing most significant byte at lowest memory address.

questions.question: What is the most accurate description of 'Big Endian'?
options: unit directing instruction execution and control signals
larger but slower cache than L1
storing most significant byte at lowest memory address
interrupt that cannot be ignored by CPU
questions.explanation: Big Endian refers to: storing most significant byte at lowest memory address.

questions.question: 'Big Endian' belongs mainly to which architecture area?
questions.explanation: Big Endian is mainly part of Architecture Design.

questions.question: A system design requirement states: storing most significant byte at lowest memory address. Which concept should be selected?
questions.explanation: The requirement directly points to Big Endian.

questions.question: Which Computer Architecture concept best matches: storing least significant byte at lowest memory address?
questions.explanation: Little Endian is defined as: storing least significant byte at lowest memory address.

questions.question: What is the most accurate description of 'Little Endian'?
options: condition causing pipeline stall or incorrect execution
storing least significant byte at lowest memory address
shared cache across multiple cores in many CPUs
byte order used for storing multi-byte data
questions.explanation: Little Endian refers to: storing least significant byte at lowest memory address.

questions.question: 'Little Endian' belongs mainly to which architecture area?
questions.explanation: Little Endian is mainly part of Architecture Design.

questions.question: A system design requirement states: storing least significant byte at lowest memory address. Which concept should be selected?
questions.explanation: The requirement directly points to Little Endian.

questions.question: Which Computer Architecture concept best matches: programmer-visible interface of processor operations?
questions.explanation: Instruction Set Architecture is defined as: programmer-visible interface of processor operations.

questions.question: What is the most accurate description of 'Instruction Set Architecture'?
options: number of CPU cycles executed per second
CPU design issuing multiple instructions per clock
separate memory paths for instructions and data
questions.explanation: Instruction Set Architecture refers to: programmer-visible interface of processor operations.

questions.question: 'Instruction Set Architecture' belongs mainly to which architecture area?
questions.explanation: Instruction Set Architecture is mainly part of Architecture Design.

questions.question: A system design requirement states: programmer-visible interface of processor operations. Which concept should be selected?
questions.explanation: The requirement directly points to Instruction Set Architecture.

questions.question: Which Computer Architecture concept best matches: hardware unit translating virtual addresses to physical addresses?
questions.explanation: MMU is defined as: hardware unit translating virtual addresses to physical addresses.

questions.question: What is the most accurate description of 'MMU'?
options: closest and fastest cache level to CPU core
I/O method using memory address space for devices
communication pathway carrying data, address, and control signals
hardware unit translating virtual addresses to physical addresses
questions.explanation: MMU refers to: hardware unit translating virtual addresses to physical addresses.

questions.question: 'MMU' belongs mainly to which architecture area?
questions.explanation: MMU is mainly part of Architecture Design.

questions.question: A system design requirement states: hardware unit translating virtual addresses to physical addresses. Which concept should be selected?
questions.explanation: The requirement directly points to MMU.
